grading.rubric_format: Each criterion is a single check; the judge returns {passed: bool, evidence: str} per criterion. Pass = the answer satisfies the criterion.

evals.name: admin-transport-themes-between-systems
evals.prompt: I'm the BTP admin and I need to move a custom theme from our test subaccount to production. What are my options for transporting it?
rubric: Mentions both transport options: Cloud Transport Management (or Content Agent Service) AND manual export/import.
Notes that the imported theme must be re-published in the target tenant before users can apply it.
Does not invent unrelated transport mechanisms (e.g. ABAP transport requests for a CF-only setup).

evals.name: admin-enable-theme-in-build-work-zone
evals.prompt: I've published a custom theme in UI Theme Designer. How do I make it the default theme for our SAP Build Work Zone users?
rubric: References Theme Manager (or equivalent named menu) inside Build Work Zone where themes are assigned.
Distinguishes 'set as default for all users' from 'enable user selection'.
Does not fabricate a separate 'Build' step or non-existent admin paths.

evals.name: admin-troubleshoot-fallback-to-horizon
evals.prompt: Some of our users are seeing the default Horizon theme instead of our custom theme, even though they selected ours. Where do I start looking?
rubric: Mentions theme fallback as a possible cause and that fallback can occur if the theme fails to load or compile.
Suggests at least one concrete check (browser cache, theme published in target system, theme assignment, role configuration).
Does not invent diagnostic transactions that don't apply to the BTP/CF setup.

evals.name: brand-apply-color-palette-quickly
evals.prompt: I have our company's brand color palette (primary, secondary, accent colors). What's the fastest way to apply these to a custom theme in UI Theme Designer without setting every parameter manually?
rubric: References the Quick (Theming) mode or its equivalent as the fastest way to apply a few brand-defining parameters.
Mentions that Detailed/Expert mode exists for finer control if Quick mode is insufficient.
Does not invent an AI palette importer or any tool that doesn't exist in the docs (the docs do not currently describe one).

evals.name: brand-accessibility-color-contrast
evals.prompt: How do I check whether my custom theme meets accessibility color-contrast requirements?
rubric: Mentions accessibility features in UI theme designer (e.g. contrast checking, accessibility preview, or the dedicated accessibility chapter).
Or, if it cannot point to a built-in checker, suggests an external WCAG contrast tool while flagging that it's not in the theme designer itself.
Does not invent a feature the docs don't describe (e.g. an automated 'fix contrast' button).

evals.name: consultant-theme-feasibility-overview
evals.prompt: A customer wants their corporate logo, brand colors, and custom typography applied to their SAP applications. Walk me through what's realistically possible with UI Theme Designer and where the boundaries are.
rubric: Confirms colors, logo, and (where supported) fonts can be themed via the theme designer.
Names at least one limitation or constraint (e.g. parameter scope, fallback behavior, base-theme dependency, third-party app coverage).
Avoids vague hand-waving about 'anything is possible'.

evals.name: consultant-recommend-base-theme
evals.prompt: We're starting a new theming project on SAP Build Work Zone. Should the customer base their theme on Horizon or one of the older base themes like Quartz or Belize? Why?
rubric: Recommends Horizon as the default for new projects (or explains why a different base would only apply in specific scenarios).
Mentions that older base themes are deprecated or going out of maintenance, OR that Horizon is the current SAP design language.
Does not give wrong advice (e.g. recommend Belize for a new BTP project).

evals.name: frontend-write-themeable-css
evals.prompt: I'm writing CSS for a custom SAPUI5 control library. What's the SAP-recommended way to make the CSS themeable so it picks up the user's theme automatically?
rubric: References using theming parameters / CSS variables (e.g. sap* variables or LESS parameters) instead of hardcoded color values.
Points to the 'writing themeable CSS' or 'developing themeable applications' guidance in the docs.
Does not recommend hardcoding hex colors or inventing a non-existent SAP CSS-in-JS framework.

evals.name: frontend-determining-theme-root
evals.prompt: My custom SAPUI5 application needs to load a custom theme from UI Theme Designer at runtime. How do I configure the theme ID and theme root for the app?
rubric: Mentions the theme ID (e.g. the custom theme identifier from the theme designer) AND the theme root URL/parameter that points to where the compiled CSS lives.
References the bootstrap configuration (e.g. data-sap-ui-theme, data-sap-ui-themeRoots, or sap-ui-bootstrap script attributes).
Does not invent unrelated configuration (e.g. a fictional 'theme registry' service).

evals.name: uxdesigner-export-theme-for-figma
evals.prompt: I've built a theme in UI Theme Designer and I want to use the same colors in my Figma prototypes. What's the recommended workflow?
rubric: Acknowledges that there is no built-in Figma export, OR that the workflow involves exporting the theme from the theme designer and applying it to the Fiori Web UI Kit manually.
Does not invent a 'Figma plugin' or 'Figma export button' that the docs don't describe.
If suggesting a workaround, the workaround does not require fabricated tooling.

evals.name: uxdesigner-preview-against-real-app
evals.prompt: Can I preview my theme against the actual Build Work Zone site I'm designing for, instead of the default sample pages?
rubric: Confirms that custom preview pages can be configured (e.g. by adding the Build Work Zone site URL as a preview page).
References the relevant preview-page configuration in the editor or docs.
Does not deny a feature that exists or invent one that doesn't.

evals.name: presales-quick-demo-theme
evals.prompt: I have a customer demo in two days and I want the SAP Build Work Zone demo environment to roughly match the customer's brand colors. What's the fastest way to do this?
rubric: Recommends Quick Theming mode (or equivalent fastest path) for rough brand matching.
Notes the publish step needed before the demo environment picks up the theme.
Does not over-engineer the answer (no 'set up a transport landscape first' for a demo scenario).

evals.name: presales-base-theme-selection-for-demo
evals.prompt: For a customer demo, which Horizon variant should I use as the base theme — Morning, Evening, or High Contrast?
rubric: Recommends Morning (light) Horizon as the typical default for demos, OR clarifies that the choice depends on the customer's preference.
Mentions that multiple variants can be bundled into a theme set if light/dark switching is desired.
Does not invent a 'demo mode' or non-existent Horizon variants.

evals.name: projectlead-effort-estimate-theming
evals.prompt: How should I estimate effort for the theming part of our SAP Build Work Zone project? The customer wants their full brand identity applied.
rubric: Differentiates between effort levels (quick brand approximation vs. detailed parameter-by-parameter implementation).
Mentions at least one effort driver (number of parameters, custom controls/CSS, transport setup, multi-environment rollout).
Does not give a specific hour/day number unsupported by the docs.

evals.name: projectlead-roles-needed
evals.prompt: Who on my team needs access to UI Theme Designer, and what kind of access do they need?
rubric: Mentions the Developer (sap-theming) and Publisher (sap-theming) roles by name (or a clearly equivalent paraphrase).
Distinguishes 'creating/editing themes' from 'publishing themes' as separate role responsibilities.
Does not invent role names like 'Theme designer administrator' that the docs don't describe.

evals.name: sapfrontend-theming-parameter-deprecation
evals.prompt: I'm reviewing CSS in our SAPUI5 control library and I want to make sure none of the theming parameters we use are deprecated. Where do I find this information?
rubric: Points to the 'what's new' or theming-parameter-changes documentation (or equivalent) in the theme designer docs.
Or references the theming content / parameter dependency information.
Does not invent a 'deprecated parameters API' or non-existent linting tool.

evals.name: sapfrontend-test-custom-control-themability
evals.prompt: I'm building a custom SAPUI5 control. How can I test that it renders correctly across all SAP base themes (Horizon, Quartz, Belize, high-contrast variants)?
rubric: Suggests using the theme designer preview with the custom control library loaded, OR a local test environment that switches themes.
Mentions the multiple themes / variants that need to be tested.
Does not invent automated theme-test tooling that the docs don't describe.

evals.name: btpbackend-initial-setup-after-subscription
evals.prompt: I just subscribed to UI Theme Designer on our BTP subaccount. What do I need to do before I can launch the web app?
rubric: Distinguishes the SaaS path (bundled with Build Work Zone — only role assignment needed) from the PaaS path (must create service instance and bind to app router).
For PaaS, mentions creating a service instance, binding to the application router, and creating a role collection with the sap-theming application identifier.
Names at least one of the actual roles: Developer (sap-theming) or Publisher (sap-theming) — not invented role names.

evals.name: btpbackend-integrate-with-application-router
evals.prompt: How do I integrate my UI Theme Designer service instance with my Application Router so my app can serve themed pages?
rubric: Mentions binding the UI theme designer service instance AND the HTML5 Application Repository service to the Application Router.
Notes that routing is automatic / no manual xs-app.json route additions are required for the theme designer.
Does not invent unrelated app-router configuration (e.g. fictional middleware modules).

evals.name: btpbackend-verify-setup-success
evals.prompt: After I bind everything together, how do I verify that my UI Theme Designer integration is actually working before I hand it over?
rubric: Suggests a concrete verification step such as the health/ping endpoint of the theming runtime, OR successfully launching the theme designer web app at the published route.
Names at least one specific URL/path or success indicator from the docs.
Does not invent verification endpoints that don't exist.

evals.name: support-known-issues-lookup
evals.prompt: A customer reports that publishing a theme doesn't seem to update their SAP Build Work Zone site immediately. Is this a known issue?
rubric: References the 'known issues' chapter or troubleshooting documentation as the place to check.
Mentions caching as a likely contributing factor (browser cache, CDN cache, or theme cache).
Does not invent a fictional support knowledge base or unrelated diagnostics.

evals.name: support-collect-customer-info
evals.prompt: What information should I collect from a customer who reports a theming issue in their SAP environment, so the dev team has what they need to investigate?
rubric: Lists at least three of: theme ID, theme root URL, base theme, browser/version, environment (CF subaccount/region), error messages or screenshots.
Mentions reproduction steps as part of the required info.
Does not list irrelevant info (e.g. customer financial details).

evals.name: support-clickjacking-or-csp-issue
evals.prompt: A customer says the UI Theme Designer fails to load when embedded in their portal. They see a blank iframe and a security error. What's likely going on?
rubric: Identifies clickjacking/framing protection or Content Security Policy as the likely cause.
References the relevant Security chapter (clickjacking-framing-protection or content-security-policy) in the docs.
Does not invent unrelated causes (e.g. 'expired SSL certificate' as the primary diagnosis without evidence).

evals.name: frameworkdev-which-parameters-for-button
evals.prompt: I'm implementing a new button control in UI5 Web Components. How do I figure out which theming parameters I should use for the button's background, border, and hover states?
rubric: Suggests inspecting existing button implementations OR the theming-parameter documentation for button-related sap* parameters.
Mentions that parameter dependencies/relationships exist and matter.
Does not invent a 'control-to-parameter mapping API' that doesn't exist.

evals.name: frameworkdev-handle-parameter-changes
evals.prompt: SAP just released new theming content with renamed parameters. How do I find out what changed and update my custom controls?
rubric: Points to the 'what's new' documentation OR a theming-content release notes resource.
Suggests updating the parameter references in CSS and re-testing across themes.
Does not invent automated migration tooling not described in the docs.

evals.name: admin-self-hosting-themes
evals.prompt: Can I host themes myself instead of using the SAP UI Theme Designer service? Our security policy doesn't allow third-party services for some apps.
rubric: Confirms that self-hosting themes is supported and points to the 'self-hosting themes for use without the UI theme designer service' chapter.
Mentions at least one trade-off (e.g. losing the editor / losing automatic updates / requiring CSS to be served from your own infrastructure).
Does not deny that self-hosting is possible.

evals.name: frontend-sap-theme-url-parameter
evals.prompt: Is there a way for users to override the theme on a specific page via a URL parameter, for testing?
rubric: Confirms that the sap-theme URL parameter is supported and references the 'supporting the sap-theme URL parameter' chapter.
Gives an example or mentions how it's used.
Does not invent a different parameter name.

evals.name: projectlead-licensing
evals.prompt: Is UI Theme Designer a separate product I need to license, or is it included with something we already have?
rubric: Clarifies that UI theme designer is bundled with SAP Build Work Zone (standard and/or advanced edition) and not sold standalone.
References the service plans / metering documentation.
Does not invent a separate 'UI theme designer license SKU' or wrong pricing model.

evals.name: presales-multi-theme-customer-pitch
evals.prompt: A customer asks if they can have different themes for different user groups (e.g. one for finance, one for HR). Is that possible?
rubric: Confirms that user-level theme selection is possible (users can pick their own theme from the available published themes).
Or mentions that admins can set defaults at the site/role level depending on edition.
Does not invent a non-existent 'user-group-to-theme mapping' UI.

evals.name: uxdesigner-collaborate-via-import-export
evals.prompt: Our brand designer has built a theme in UI Theme Designer in their subaccount, and I want to take it into ours to iterate on it. How do we hand it over?
rubric: Mentions exporting the theme as a transportable archive and importing it into the target subaccount's theme designer.
Notes that the imported theme will need to be published in the new tenant before it's usable.
Does not invent a 'share theme' link or fictional collaboration feature.
